title: Maha Mrityunjay Temple
tagline: Maha Mrityunjay Temple — spiritual town of Assam

overview.short: Maha Mrityunjay Temple is a Hindu Temple dedicated to Hindu God Shiva, situated in Nagaon, Assam, India.
overview.description: Maha Mrityunjay Temple is a Hindu Temple dedicated to Hindu God Shiva, situated in Nagaon, Assam, India. This Temple is special in its architectural sense as it is built in a form a Shivling. It is the World's largest Shivalinga, at the height of 126 foot. This feature is made it unique and very attractive for the devotees.

topPlaces.name: Mahapurusha Srimanta Sankaradeva Viswavidyalaya
topPlaces.description: Mahapurusha Srimanta Sankaradeva Viswavidyalaya (MSSV) is a private university located in Nagaon, Assam, India. It was established through The Mahapurusha Srimanta Sankaradeva Viswavidyalaya Act, 2013 enacted by the Assam Legislative Assembly.

topPlaces.name: Nagaon
topPlaces.description: Nagaon is a city and a municipal board in Nagaon district in the Indian state of Assam. It is situated 122.6 kilometres (76 mi) east of Guwahati.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Maha Mrityunjay Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Maha Mrityunjay Temple is Oct – Mar. Summers are 25–42°C and winters are 7–24°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Maha Mrityunjay Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Shillong Airport (Umroi) (107 km) and the nearest railway station is Guwahati Railway Station (104 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Maha Mrityunjay Temple?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Maha Mrityunjay Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Maha Mrityunjay Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Maha Mrityunjay Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Maha Mrityunjay Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Puranigudam, Choto Haibor, Mahapurusha Srimanta Sankaradeva Viswavidyalaya, Nagaon.
